title: Best VPN for Torrenting in 2026: Fast, Private, and P2P-Friendly
description: The best VPNs for torrenting in 2026 support P2P traffic, have kill switches, and do not log activity. Here are the top picks tested for torrent use.
content:
What makes a VPN good for torrenting?

A good torrenting VPN needs: P2P support on at least some servers (some providers block P2P entirely or restrict it to specific servers), a working kill switch that cuts internet if the VPN drops, no activity logs, and fast enough speeds to make downloads practical. Speed is especially important for torrenting since even a 50 percent speed reduction can make large downloads frustrating.

NordVPN: fast and P2P-optimized

NordVPN has dedicated P2P servers optimized for torrent traffic. The kill switch works at the system level. Their no-log policy has been independently audited and validated in real-world seizure tests. Speed on NordVPN P2P servers is among the fastest available. Split tunneling lets you route only your torrent client through the VPN. Price: from around 3.50 EUR per month on multi-year plans.

ExpressVPN: all servers support P2P

ExpressVPN supports torrenting on all servers, not just dedicated P2P ones. This gives you more server options for finding fast connections. The Network Lock kill switch is reliable. Speed is generally fast. More expensive than NordVPN but the flexibility of P2P on all servers is a genuine advantage.

Mullvad: best for anonymity

Mullvad supports P2P on most servers and is the most anonymous option available. No email required to sign up, cash and cryptocurrency accepted, random account numbers instead of usernames. If privacy is your primary concern for torrenting, Mullvad is the strongest choice. Price: flat 5 EUR per month. Speed is good but not the fastest among premium VPNs.

Private Internet Access (PIA): large server network for torrenting

PIA supports P2P on all servers and has the largest server network of any major VPN. More server options mean more chances to find a fast connection near you. PIA has been through legal challenges without producing user data. Price: from around 2 EUR per month on long-term plans. Good value for torrent-heavy users.

What to avoid

Free VPNs universally restrict or block P2P traffic. Even paid VPNs that advertise torrenting support sometimes throttle speeds on torrent connections or restrict P2P to slow servers in inconvenient locations. Check user reviews specific to torrent use before committing to a subscription.

Legal note

A VPN does not make illegal downloads legal. Using a VPN to torrent copyrighted material without authorization is still infringement in most jurisdictions. VPNs add privacy and security to legal activities including downloading public domain files, Linux ISOs, legal torrents, and other legitimate P2P content.
